content: Hello everyone, In the std library (Rust) there is the TcpStream::connect function but i didn't find it in the kernel environnement there's only the read and write. Is there another way to connect?
There is no TcpStream in the kernel at all. Perhaps you're looking at the old, abandoned rust branch?

Alice Ryhl said:

There is no TcpStream in the kernel at all. Perhaps you're looking at the old, abandoned rust branch?

Not upstream in Rust anyway. @Michele Dalle Rive did a patch set a while back, it is available rebased on Linux 6.7.

Andreas Hindborg said:

Alice Ryhl said:

There is no TcpStream in the kernel at all. Perhaps you're looking at the old, abandoned rust branch?

Not upstream in Rust anyway. Michele Dalle Rive did a patch set a while back, it is available rebased on Linux 6.7.

where can i find this patch ?

Mahdi Guesmi said:

Andreas Hindborg said:

Alice Ryhl said:

There is no TcpStream in the kernel at all. Perhaps you're looking at the old, abandoned rust branch?

Not upstream in Rust anyway. Michele Dalle Rive did a patch set a while back, it is available rebased on Linux 6.7.

where can i find this patch ?

Someone rebased them for nbd https://github.com/metaspace/linux/tree/rust-nbd.
